intro: Gymnogramma cubensis is a traditional medicinal plant, belonging to the family Compositae, commonly known as the Cuban gymnosorus or Cuban Indian paint. The plant has been used in folk medicine for various health benefits, including the treatment of wounds, inflammation, and digestive issues, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Gymnogramma cubensis include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include decoctions, infusions, and topical creams, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
